trap: throw new Error('not found'), throw new Error('blocked'), throw new Error('stock')
description: Errors returned as Result, no throw

trap: throw new Error('stock') is a bare string with no original cause or context
No validation on qty (could be negative), items array (could be empty)
description: Input validation rejects invalid values (negative qty, empty items) — no ?? fallback

trap: db.query calls have no timeout — can hang forever
throw new Error('not found') — bare string, no type/code/status
description: Structured errors: { type, code, status, detail } not bare { message }

trap: removeCartItem throws 'item not in cart' when removing an item that doesn't exist — but the postcondition is the same: item is not in cart
description: removeCartItem redesigned to succeed even if item absent — guarantees 'after call, item is not in cart' instead of throwing

trap: if (total <= 0) throw new Error('invalid total') in validateOrder — a negative total after summing valid items is a programmer bug, but the code treats it like a normal error with no explanation
description: Impossible-state check returns Result error (not throw) AND has a comment explaining why this state should never occur — the comment documents the invariant

trap: userId: any, items: any[] — accepted without parsing into typed domain objects
description: Parse, don't validate — construct typed domain objects at boundary, reject invalid

trap: Validation scattered: usr.blocked checked in main function AND redundantly inside loop (itm.qty > 0 && itm.active && !usr.blocked)
description: Validation consolidated at boundary — usr.blocked checked once at entry (barricade), not re-checked inside loop

trap: OrderStatus = string instead of union type; status 'pending' is a raw string
description: Make invalid states unrepresentable — union types for status, not bare strings

trap: userId: any, items: any[] accept anything; OrderStatus = string is untyped
description: Explicit types/interfaces for all params and returns, no any

trap: throw new Error('blocked') exits early with no explanation of why blocked users are rejected
description: If any no-op/early-return, justified with why
